digest.top_line: DeLLphi-304: tarlatamab CNS PFS 6.5 vs 4.2mo (HR 0.40) in SCLC brain mets, first DLL3xCD3 intracranial signal but post hoc.
digest.tldr: Thoracic and lower-GI led, both pitting molecular tools against RT-adjacent decisions. Tarlatamab's post-hoc CNS signal in SCLC (HR 0.40) is the first DLL3xCD3 intracranial read, historically WBRT/SRS/PCI territory. ctDNA in post-TNT rectal NOM misses 59% of local regrowths, augmenting but not replacing endoscopy/MRI.

sites.intro: ctDNA surveillance in organ-preservation rectal cancer: strong for distant mets, weak for the local regrowth watch-and-wait actually hinges on.

studies.name: ctDNA and Local Regrowth/Distant Mets in Nonoperative Management of Stage II-III Rectal Cancer
studies.tldr: ctDNA sensitivity 41% for local regrowth vs 74% for distant mets; positive result predicts both but can't replace endoscopy/MRI surveillance in rectal NOM.
details: 🔍 INTERCEPT program: prospective single-center cohort, N=110 MSS rectal adenoca in cCR/nCR after NAT, tumor-informed Signatera Exome
📐 Ever-positive longitudinal ctDNA → worse regrowth-free and distant-met-free survival (Fig 1, log-rank p=0.0002 and p<0.0001)
📐 First post-NAT ctDNA (n=12 evaluable, within 180d): positive → worse regrowth-free and distant-met-free survival (Fig 2, p=0.0006 and p<0.0001)
🩻 ctDNA+ at local regrowth → more advanced salvage pathology: ypT3-4 in 75% (6/8) vs 21% (3/14), p=0.01
⚠️ Diagnostic estimates rest on small event counts: 29 regrowth-associated and 42 distant-met-associated samples
⚠️ NAT was almost entirely TNT (104/110); may not transfer to short-course-RT or chemoRT-only NOM pathways
🔗 The RT read: 41% sensitivity means ctDNA can't replace DRE/endoscopy/MRI surveillance in post-TNT watch-and-wait, only augment it

verdict.rationale: Single-center prospective cohort (N=110), no interventional comparator; prognostic association consistent with emerging ctDNA-in-NOM data and reaffirms imaging/endoscopy surveillance can't be replaced.
verdict.audience: MSS stage II-III rectal adenoca in cCR/nCR on watch-and-wait after TNT

studies.significance: The asymmetry is the actionable read: ctDNA caught only 41% of local regrowths vs 74% of distant mets, so for post-TNT organ-preservation surveillance it flags distant-met risk but can't replace endoscopy/MRI. ctDNA+ at regrowth also tracked more advanced ypT3-4 salvage pathology (75% vs 21%).
studies.monday_clinic: Applies to the MSS stage II-III rectal pt in cCR/nCR pursuing watch-and-wait after TNT, in whom a negative ctDNA still can't exclude local regrowth; it does not extend to MSI-H tumors or pts not pursuing organ preservation.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: **Prospective single-center cohort** (MD Anderson INTERCEPT program), N=110, 2020-2024. Serial **tumor-informed ctDNA** (Signatera Exome), **669 samples**; median follow-up **25 mo** (IQR 18-37).

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: **MSS stage II-III rectal adenocarcinoma** in **cCR (69) or nCR (41)** after NAT, managed non-operatively. Median age 56; cT3 in 72 and cN1-2 in 60 of 110; NAT almost entirely **TNT (104/110)**.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: Regrowth-free and distant-metastasis-free survival by longitudinal and first-post-NAT ctDNA; per-sample diagnostic accuracy (±90d of each draw); salvage-surgery pathology by ctDNA status. **No registered primary endpoint** (observational).

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: Local regrowth in **23 (21%)**, distant mets in **12 (11%)**. Ever-positive ctDNA tracked worse regrowth-free and distant-met-free survival (log-rank p=0.0002 and p<0.0001). Per-sample sensitivity **41% local regrowth vs 74% distant mets**.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: Consistent with the emerging **ctDNA-as-prognostic** signal in rectal NOM, but quantifies a **sensitivity ceiling (41%)** that limits its use for local-regrowth surveillance.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents MSS stage II-III rectal adenocarcinoma in cCR/nCR pursuing organ preservation after TNT. Does not represent MSI-H tumors, operative-intent pts, or short-course-RT / chemoRT-only NOM pathways.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Single-center, N=110 with only **29 regrowth-** and **42 distant-met-associated** samples driving diagnostic estimates. Assay-specific (Signatera tumor-informed); no comparison vs imaging-alone surveillance.

open_questions: Does ctDNA-guided surveillance improve organ-preservation outcomes vs imaging alone
Can serial ctDNA kinetics raise local regrowth sensitivity above 41%
Generalizability beyond single-center tumor-informed Signatera cohort

related_trials.brief_title: ctDNA Monitoring to Predict the Efficacy of TNT for Rectal Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary:
This is a prospective observational study with three primary objectives:

Objective 1: Evaluate the detection rate and changes in circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) levels in blood samples from colorectal cancer patients before, during, and after total neoadjuvant therapy (TNT).

Determine the detection rate and change of CtDNA in blood samples of cancer patients before, during, and after TNT then assess changes in ctDNA expression within the study population during treatment.

* Determine the ctDNA…

Inclusion Criteria:

* 18 years and older, both genders
* Patients are diagnosed with stage II-III rectal cancer and indicated for total neoadjuvant therapy
* Biopsy FFPE sample is available at the time of diagnosis
* Patients consented to participate in the study

Exclusion Criteria:

* Stage I…
related_trials.answers_question: Does ctDNA-guided surveillance improve organ-preservation outcomes vs imaging alone

sites.intro: A systemic bispecific posts an intracranial signal in SCLC, RT's historic domain for CNS control.

studies.name: DeLLphi-304
studies.tldr: Intracranial: time-to-CNS-progression HR 0.54 ITT; in brain-mets pts CNS PFS 6.5 vs 4.2mo (HR 0.40), CNS CR 15% vs 5% for tarlatamab.
details: 🔍 Post hoc intracranial analysis of DeLLphi-304 (phase 3, 2L SCLC tarlatamab vs chemo); CNS endpoints not a prespecified primary
🔍 ITT CNS cohort n=254 vs 255; brain-mets subgroup n=67 vs 56 (mRANO-BM by BICR)
⚠️ >70% of brain-mets pts had prior CNS-directed therapy: salvage intracranial activity, not de novo control
⚠️ Brain-mets HRs unstratified, small N, post hoc; hypothesis-generating
📊 CNS tumor shrinkage ≥30%: 56.3% (9/16) tarlatamab vs 38.5% (5/13) chemo
🔗 First DLL3xCD3 bispecific intracranial signal in SCLC; historically CNS control leaned on WBRT/SRS and PCI

verdict.rationale: Post hoc CNS analysis; brain-mets HRs unstratified with small N (67 vs 56), and >70% had prior CNS-directed therapy, confounding de novo intracranial activity.
verdict.audience: Relapsed SCLC with brain mets, >70% prior CNS-directed therapy

studies.significance: The RT read is intracranial control without new radiation: brain-mets CNS PFS 6.5 vs 4.2mo (HR 0.40) and CNS CR 15% vs 5%. But >70% had prior CNS-directed therapy, so this is salvage intracranial activity, not a defer-brain-RT signal in RT-naive disease. Informs sequencing systemic ahead of repeat cranial RT.
studies.monday_clinic: For relapsed SCLC with previously-treated brain mets, this supports systemic intracranial control as an option before committing to repeat cranial RT; it does not inform RT-naive or symptomatic brain mets, where upfront local therapy still applies.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: **Post hoc** intracranial analysis of DeLLphi-304, a phase 3 RCT of tarlatamab vs chemotherapy in relapsed SCLC. CNS endpoints were **not prespecified primary**. Data cutoff Jan 29 2025; median follow-up 11.4 mo (tarlatamab), 11.5 mo (chemo).

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: Relapsed SCLC. ITT CNS cohort n=254 tarlatamab vs 255 chemo; brain-mets subgroup n=67 vs 56. **>70% of brain-mets pts had prior CNS-directed therapy.**

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: Time to CNS progression or death (ITT, RECIST per investigator); brain-mets CNS PFS by **mRANO-BM (BICR)**; best CNS response, CNS disease control rate, and response durations.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: ITT time to CNS progression **HR 0.54 (0.39-0.75)**, median NE vs 7.2 mo. Brain-mets CNS PFS and response detail in figures.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: First **DLL3xCD3 bispecific** intracranial signal in SCLC; CNS control historically relied on WBRT/SRS and prophylactic cranial irradiation, not a systemic agent.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents relapsed SCLC with brain mets, mostly previously treated for CNS disease. Does not represent RT-naive or symptomatic brain mets needing upfront local therapy.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Post hoc, **unstratified** brain-mets HRs, small subgroup N. >70% prior CNS therapy confounds de novo intracranial activity. No new safety data in source.

open_questions: Durability of intracranial response beyond current follow-up
Activity in RT-naive or untreated brain metastases
Sequencing tarlatamab vs cranial radiotherapy in SCLC brain mets

2. Age 18 years or older
3. Patients with pathology proven metastatic SCLC

related_trials.brief_title: Tarlatamab for the Treatment of Extensive Stage Small-cell Lung Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: This phase II trial tests the effect of tarlatamab in treating patients with small-cell lung cancer (SCLC) that has spread from where it first started to other parts of the body (extensive-stage). SCLC is an aggressive cancer that has a low 5-year survival rate. Tarlatamab is a bispecific antibody that can bind to two different antigens at the same time. Tarlatamab binds to DLL3, a protein found on the surface of some types of tumor cells, including small-cell lung cancer, and to CD3, which is…
conditions: Extensive Stage Lung Small Cell Carcinoma

image_ocr_texts:
Association Between Circulating Tumor DNA and Local Tumor Regrowth and Distant Metastasis
During Nonoperative Management in Stage H-III Rectal Cancer
Kentaro Ochiai, Megan E. Delisle, Alisha H Bent, Van K. Morris, Arvind Dasari, Emma B. Holliday, Timothy E Newhook, Kristin Alfaro, Kathryn Aziz, Paula Marincola Smith, Michael G. White, Y. Nancy You,
Brian K. Bednarski, Ramy S. Behman, Jeongyoon Moon, Craig A. Messick, George J. Chang, J. Joshua Smith, Scott Kopetz, Tsuyoshi Konishi
The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center, Houston, TX
The INTERCEPT Program
THE UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS
MD Anderson
Cancer Center
Making Cancer Hatory
Background
• During nonoperative management (NOM) for rectal cancer after a clinical
complete response (cCR) or near-cCR (nCR), 25-30% develop local
regrowth and 5-10% develop distant metastasis, highlighting the needs of
improved risk stratification and surveillance.
• Circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) has emerged as a prognostic biomarker.
but its utility in NOM decision making remains undefined.
Methods
• In the INTERCEPT program, we studied 110 patients (2020-2024) with;
• Stages I-Ili, microsatellite stable rectal adenocarcinoma
> cCR / nCR after neoadjuvant therapy (NAT) and underwent NOM
• Surveillance with tumor-informed ctDNA testing (Signatera™ Exome)
• Outcomes:
1) Local regrowth and/or distant metastasis by longitudinal cIDNA status
2) Local regrowth and/or distant metastasis by the first post-treatment
CIDNA status (within 180 days post NAT)
3) Per-sample accuracy for local regrowth and/or distant metastasis
occurring within +90 days of each blood draw
4) Pathological characteristic of salvage surgery for local regrowth by
CIDNA status
Results
Table 1. Patient characteristics
Characteristics
Conclusions
• Positive ctDNA predicts local regrowth and distant recurrence after NOM for rectal cancer
• Sensitivity of ctDNA for detecting local regrowth is limited
ctDNA should be integrated as an adjunct tool with imaging and clinical assessments
Results
1) Patients with ever positive longitudinal ctDNA had an associated worse 2-year local regrowth-free and metastasis-free survival than those with persistentiy negative ctDNA (Figure 1)|
2)
Among patients with an evaluable first post-treatment ctDNA (n=12), those with a positive result had an associated lower regrowth-free and metastasis-free survival (Figure 2)
In per-sample analysis (n=669), ctDNA showed low sensitivity and high specificity for local regrowth and higher sensitivity and specificity for distant metastasis (Table 2)
4) Twenty-two of 23 patients with local regrowth underwent salvage surgery; cIDNA positivity at local regrowth was associated with more advanced pathological T stage (Table 3)

Tarlatamab was associated with a better intracranial complete response rate, duration of intracranial complete response,
and duration of intracranial disease control compared to chemotherapy.
At the data-cutoff date of January 29, 2025, the median study follow-up was 11.4 months (tarlatarnab) and 11.5 months (chemotherapy). + denotes censored data.
*As most patients (>70%) had received prior treatment for CNS disease, specified outcomes were CR, non-CR/non-PD, and PD.
*Assessed in patients with 2 1 lesion that was 2 10mm in diameter.
Cl, confidence interval; CNS, central nervous system; CR, complete response; N1, number of patients with CNS lesions ≥ 10mm in diameter; NE, not estimable; PD, progressive disease.
